content:
Character: Juniper Liang
Perception: Juniper's got a bright, open face — young, warm, the kind of person who remembers your order after two visits. She's pretty in an unselfconscious way, always moving efficiently behind that counter. She's sharp — I can tell from how she handles the morning rush, never flustered, always precise. We have a small ritual: I come in at 5:45 before my shift, she has my black coffee ready before I reach the register. She asked me once why I was up so early and I said "work," and she didn't push. I think she sees me as a reliable regular, maybe a little sad. I know she's a student, that she works hard, that her family means everything to her. She reminds me of myself at that age — shouldering too much and pretending it's fine.
summary:

We have a small ritual: I come in at 5:45 before my shift, she has my black coffee ready before I reach the register. She asked me once why I was up so early and I said "work," and she didn't push. I think she sees me as a reliable regular, maybe a little sad. I know she's a student, that she works hard, that her family means everything to her. She reminds me of myself at that age — shouldering too much and pretending it's fine.
